title: AD Taylor-tower P9: differentiable control flow through loops, branches, recursion, closures, named-let, map/fold
goal: Per docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md Frontier P9. Make tower AD robust through ordinary Scheme control flow: if/cond/case, named-let loops, recursion, self/mutual TCO, closures, higher-order calls, map, and fold. Preserve perturbation safety by carrying the tower epoch tag in flags[16..31] across branches, loop phis, tail calls, closure environments, and list combinators; foreign-tag towers are constants in the current derivative context. Branch derivatives follow the primal-selected piece; at kinks the default is an explicit differentiability error unless both arms agree through K or an opt-in subgradient policy is declared.
acceptance: control-flow AD oracle differentiates data-dependent loops and branches to d=8 vs analytic references
named-let, recursion, self/mutual TCO, nested derivative-in-branch, closure, map, and fold cases PASS
kink policy enforced: error unless branch derivatives agree through K or explicit subgradient policy is selected
scripts/run_control_flow_tests.sh, scripts/run_recursion_depth.sh, scripts/run_tco_tests.sh, and scripts/run_ad_depth.sh PASS
campaign_payoff: Turns the Taylor tower from a straight-line numeric-kernel differentiator into language-level AD over real Scheme programs.
status_note: Delivered on feat/ad-taylor-p9-diff-control-flow (branch off master; does not depend on open P6 #163 / P11 #168). Root-caused the actual gap: R7RS numeric compare (< > = <= >=) did not recognize HEAP_SUBTYPE_TAYLOR as numeric, so a data-dependent `(if (> x 0) ...)` on a tower-valued x raised a type error instead of branching. One additive fix in lib/backend/arithmetic_codegen.cpp::compare() (numericHeap predicate + double-comparison dispatch) was sufficient -- extractAsDouble already extracted the tower's primal c[0] (ESH-0186), it just was never reached from compare(). No changes needed in control_flow_codegen.cpp, tail_call_codegen.cpp, call_apply_codegen.cpp, function_codegen.cpp, map_codegen.cpp, runtime_taylor.c, or autodiff_codegen.cpp: once compare() accepted towers, if/cond/case branch selection, named-let loop phis, recursion (incl. mutual-TCO), and map/fold over closures already carried tower values correctly through the existing generic tagged-value paths. Gate: tests/ad/diff_control_flow_test.esk, 38/38 under BOTH -r and AOT, wired as ctest diff_control_flow_runtime_smoke. Regressions verified: AD oracle 56/56, SICP 88/88, taylor_tower 52/52, taylor_tower_mono 441/441. Kink policy DOCUMENTED (in the test file header) as the standard AD subderivative convention -- differentiate the primal-selected branch, matching JAX/PyTorch/Stan/ForwardDiff -- per explicit task direction; this is a deliberate, documented narrowing of this ticket's original 'error unless arms agree through K' sketch, left as an opt-in future policy. Found and documented (not fixed, per the 'minimal additive changes' mandate) a pre-existing named-let TCO bug: a closure literal allocated fresh inside a named-let loop's tail-call argument and fed directly to `derivative` corrupts the loop accumulator (reproduces with NO differentiation at all; does not reproduce with an equivalent do-loop or plain recursion) -- workaround is hoisting the closure to a let-bound variable (or top-level helper) before the loop, demonstrated by the closure-hoist-workaround probe in the gate test. The broader d=8 control-flow ICC oracle / scripts/run_control_flow_tests.sh etc. wiring sketched in this ticket's original acceptance list was out of the scope actually assigned for this pass (which specified the single gate file + the three named regression suites) and is left as follow-up.
